items.title: investingLive European FX news wrap: Markets consolidate on US Juneteenth
items.summary: How have interest rate expectations changed after this week's events? ECB policymaker Wunsch says upside surprises in inflation would justify further tightening ECB policymaker Lane sees the current inflation shock as "mid-sized", defends rate hike USD/JPY is near the highest level since 1986 as divergence with the Fed intensifies UK May retail sales +1.2% vs +0.5% m/m expected FX option expiries for 19 June 10am New York cut What are the main events for today? It's been a very light session with no major data or news releases. The markets were also pretty calm with things expected to die down further during the NA session due to the US holiday. As a reminder, the US stock market and bond market will be closed and will resume trading on Monday. Futures markets typically halt or experience early closures, often closing at 12:00 p.m. ET. The only economic data we got was the UK Retail Sales report. The data surprised to the upside across the board but the market reaction was pretty much muted as it doesn't change anything for the BoE. ECB policymakers Lane and Wunsch defended last week's rate hike decision arguing that the ECB had not made a mistake by hiking while inflation was rising and uncertainty remained elevated. Wunsch, in particular, acknowledged that easing geopolitical tensions involving Iran and moderating wage growth could strengthen the argument that the ECB might have been able to look through the energy-driven inflation spike. Still, he maintained that last week’s decision was justified given the information available at the time. In the American session, there's just the Canadian Retail Sales report which is not going to change anything for the BoC, so the market reaction will likely be muted. Have a great weekend! This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 本周事件发生后，市场对利率的预期发生了哪些变化？欧洲央行决策者温施表示，通胀的上行意外将为进一步收紧货币政策提供依据；欧洲央行决策者莱恩则认为当前的通胀冲击属于“中等规模”， 并为加息辩护；随着与美联储的政策分化加剧，美元兑日元汇率逼近1986年以来的最高水平；英国5月零售销售环比增长1.2%，高于预期值0.5%；6月19日纽约时间上午10点外汇期权到期；今日有哪些主要事件？今日交易时段相当清淡，没有重大数据或新闻发布。市场也相当平静，由于美国假期，预计北美交易时段市场将进一步趋于平静。提醒一下，美国股市和债市将休市，并将于周一恢复交易。期货市场通常会暂停交易或提前收盘，往往在美东时间中午12:00收盘。我们收到的唯一经济数据是英国零售销售报告。该数据全面好于预期，但市场反应相当平淡，因为这不会对英国央行（BoE）的政策产生任何影响。 欧洲央行决策者莱恩（Lane）和温施（Wunsch）为上周的加息决定进行了辩护，他们认为，在通胀持续攀升且不确定性依然居高不下的情况下，欧洲央行选择加息并未犯错。温施特别指出，伊朗相关地缘政治紧张局势的缓和以及工资增长放缓，或许能进一步佐证这样一种观点：即欧洲央行本可以忽略由能源价格推动的通胀飙升。尽管如此，他仍坚持认为，根据当时掌握的信息，上周的决定是合理的。在美股交易时段，仅公布加拿大零售销售报告，这不会对加拿大央行（BoC）的政策产生任何影响，因此市场反应预计将较为温和。祝大家周末愉快！本文作者为investinglive.com的朱塞佩·德拉莫塔（Giuseppe Dellamotta）。

ECB policymaker Wunsch says upside surprises in inflation would justify further tightening ECB policymaker Lane sees the current inflation shock as "mid-sized", defends rate hike USD/JPY is near the highest level since 1986 as divergence with the Fed intensifies UK May retail sales +1.2% vs +0.5% m/m expected FX option expiries for 19 June 10am New York cut What are the main events for today? It's been a very light session with no major data or news releases. The markets were also pretty calm with things expected to die down further during the NA session due to the US holiday. This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.

items.title: investingLive Americas FX news wrap 18 Jun USDJPY surges to near highs going to 2024
items.summary: Russell 2000 rises by 2.12% and NASDAQ by 1.91% to lead stocks higher Trump: US is committed to PEACE Iran supreme leader responds to US MOU with caution Bitcoin price action is spelling T-R-O-U-B-L-E for the buyers US list blockade of maritime traffic entering and exiting the Straits of Hormuz Key Points from the MOU between the US and Iran. Major European indices close mixed with a bigger tilt to the upside VP Vance: We will manage flare-ups through diplomacy. Trump: Iran not a threat. Oil lower. Stocks higher. Prices dropping. Bloomberg reports that Kuwait is boosting oil output. Crude oil tests key 200 day MA US June Philly Fed business index 10.3 versus 10.0 estimate US initial jobless claims 226K vs 225K estimate. Continuing Claims 1.810M vs 1.795M est Canada May PPI +1.2% m/m vs +1.8% expected ECBs Lane: Hikes aim to contain spread of energy shock investingLive European FX news wrap: SNB and BoE steady; US dollar extends gains The USD continued its run to the upside after the more hawkish Fed yesterday. The gains in the greenback were led by gains versus the GBP (+0.68%), the CHF (+0.56%) and the JPY (+0.47%). The BOE and the SNB both kept rates unchanged. For the Bank of England, although the skew is toward higher rates, the central bank seems to be on hold for the foreseeable future as risks of slower growth balances things out a bit versus inflation. For the SNB, they indicated that intervention is not out of the question against the strong CHF. The USDJPY started to step higher after breaking above the 2026 high at 160.717 and squeezed higher as the price continued away from the 160.00 level and move to and through 161.00 instead. Anxiety about intervention reared its ugly head once the price extended up to 161.81 just about 11 or 12 pips short of the highest level going back to 2024. The price quickly move down to a low of 160.887 but then bounced back higher and is trading near 161.40 heading into the end of day. Traders can expect more anxiety going forward, but the buyers are still more in control as long at the old high for the year at 160.717 holds support. Move below it and staying below is now needed to give the sellers some control from a technical perspective back. US stocks after falling yesterday, recovered nicely today help by the chip stocks. The trading week for stocks ends today due to the Juneteenth holiday tomorrow. For the week, the major indices closed higher: Dow industrial average +0.14%. S&P index +1.08% NASDAQ index +1.91% Russell 2000 + 2.12% The US debt market will also be closed tomorrow. Yields in the short end edged higher today, while the yields in the longer end fell as the yield flattening continues. 2 year yield 4.1809%, +1.8 basis points 5 year 4.234%, +5.2 basis points 10 year 4.457%, -5.8 basis points 30 year 4.899%, -2.7 basis points For the week, the 2 year yield is up around 10.0 basis points. 10 year yield is down -2.6 basis points. Fundamentally: US initial jobless claims came in as expected at 226K vs 225K estimate Philly Fed regional index was also near expectation at 10.3 vs 10.0 estimate. . The U.S.-Iran Memorandum of Understanding was released/leaked. It showed a 14-point framework designed to end hostilities, reopen trade and energy flows, and provide a path toward a broader peace and nuclear agreement. The deal calls for an immediate halt to military operations, a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz with safe commercial passage, and the removal of the U.S. naval blockade. Both sides have agreed to negotiate a final agreement within 60 days. Iran reaffirmed that it will not develop nuclear weapons and will work with international inspectors on its nuclear program, while the U.S. pledged sanctions relief, access to frozen Iranian assets, and support for a reconstruction and economic development package worth at least $300 billion. The agreement also allows Iranian oil exports to resume, establishes a monitoring mechanism for compliance, and envisions a final deal that would be backed by a binding U.N. Security Council resolution. Markets have viewed the agreement as a major de-escalation event, helping ease concerns over oil supply disruptions and broader regional instability This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 罗素2000指数上涨2.12%，纳斯达克指数上涨1.91%，领涨大盘。特朗普：美国致力于和平。伊朗最高领袖对美伊谅解备忘录持谨慎态度。比特币价格走势预示着买家将面临“麻烦”。美国宣布封锁进出霍尔木兹海峡的海上交通。美伊谅解备忘录要点。欧洲主要股指收盘涨跌不一，整体偏向上涨 副国务卿万斯：我们将通过外交手段管控局势升级。特朗普：伊朗不构成威胁。油价下跌。股市上涨。物价走低。彭博社报道称科威特正在增加原油产量。原油价格测试关键的200日均线 美国6月费城联储商业指数为10.3，高于10.0的预期 美国初请失业金人数为22.6万，高于22.5万的预期。续请失业金人数181万，预期179.5万；加拿大5月生产者物价指数（PPI）环比+1.2%，预期+1.8%；欧洲央行莱恩：加息旨在遏制能源冲击的蔓延；investingLive欧洲外汇新闻综述：瑞士央行和英国央行维持利率不变；美元延续涨势。在美联储昨日释放更强硬信号后，美元延续了上涨势头。美元的上涨主要得益于其对英镑（+0.68%）、瑞士法郎（+0.56%）和日元（+0.47%）的升值。英国央行和瑞士央行均维持利率不变。 就英格兰银行而言，尽管市场预期倾向于加息，但鉴于经济增长放缓的风险在一定程度上抵消了通胀压力，该央行在可预见的未来似乎将维持利率不变。瑞士央行方面则表示，针对瑞士法郎走强，不排除采取干预措施的可能性。美元兑日元在突破2026年高点160.717后开始攀升，随着价格持续远离160.00关口，并进一步上行至161.00并突破该水平，汇价进一步走高。当价格升至161.81（距离2024年以来的最高点仅差11或12点）时，市场对干预的担忧再度浮现。价格迅速回落至160.887的低点，但随后反弹回升，临近收盘时交投于161.40附近。交易者预计未来市场将持续动荡，但只要年内前高160.717仍能提供支撑，多头仍占据主导地位。从技术角度看，卖方若要重新掌握主动权，价格必须跌破该水平并持续维持在下方。美国股市在昨日下跌后，今日在芯片股的带动下强劲反弹。 由于明天是“六月节”（Juneteenth）假期，本周股市交易将于今日结束。本周主要股指收高：道琼斯工业平均指数上涨0.14%，标普500指数上涨1.08%，纳斯达克指数上涨1.91%，罗素2000指数上涨2.12%。美国债券市场明天也将休市。今日短期债券收益率小幅走高，而长期债券收益率则有所回落，收益率曲线趋平的趋势仍在持续。2年期收益率4.1809%，+1.8个基点；5年期4.234%，+5.2个基点；10年期4.457%，-5.8个基点；30年期4.899%，-2.7个基点。本周，2年期收益率上涨约10.0个基点。10年期收益率下跌2.6个基点。基本面方面：美国初请失业金人数为22.6万，符合预期（市场预估22.5万）；费城联储地区指数为10.3，也接近预期（市场预估10.0）。美伊谅解备忘录已发布/泄露。该文件提出了一项14点框架，旨在结束敌对行动、恢复贸易和能源流动，并为达成更广泛的和平与核协议铺平道路。该协议要求立即停止军事行动、重新开放霍尔木兹海峡以确保商业船只安全通行，并解除美国海军封锁。 双方已同意在60天内就最终协议进行谈判。伊朗重申不会研发核武器，并将就其核计划与国际核查人员合作；而美国则承诺解除制裁、解冻伊朗资产，并支持一项价值至少3000亿美元的重建与经济发展计划。该协议还允许伊朗恢复石油出口，建立了合规监督机制，并设想最终协议将获得具有约束力的联合国安理会决议的支持。市场将该协议视为一次重大的局势缓和事件，有助于缓解对石油供应中断及更广泛地区动荡的担忧。本文作者为investinglive.com的格雷格·米哈洛夫斯基。

items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: Vance cancels trip for Iran talks
items.summary: Japan CPI stays muted in May as subsidies mask building inflation pressure Oil and US dollar rising on news that Vance cancelled his trip to negotiate with Iran BoJ deputy governor says delaying on price risks could cause long-term economic damage Vance cancels his trip to Switzerland Japan finmin says prepared to take decisive action on speculative FX moves BoJ's Deputy governor warns yen moves now carry bigger inflation punch than in the past China says Australian beef imports hit 100% of quota on June 18th BoJ April minutes: Three members wanted to move in April; the rest caught up by June Japan May inflation data: Headline 1.5% y/y (expected 1.5%) Core 1.4% (expected 1.4%) UK consumer confidence masks growing cracks beneath steady headline New Zealand May 2026 trade data, exports and imports both up from April USD/JPY mystery sell-off from 161.80 leaves traders hunting for answers Citi sees oil at $60-65 by Q1 2027 as Hormuz flows normalise Iraq flags gradual oil return as Hormuz reopens, but risks linger Reminder - Hong Kong and China markets are closed for a holiday today, 19 June 2026 After 15 years of word-watching, markets may benefit from a Fed that talks less investingLive Americas FX news wrap 18 Jun USDJPY surges to near highs going to 2024 Summary: Iran has frozen its MOU commitments and postponed the Geneva talks with the US until a Lebanon ceasefire is in place, with Israel's continued attacks triggering the deal's first clause; Vance's Switzerland cancellation followed Oil and the USD edged higher on the news; gold slipped back under $4,200 Japan's core CPI held at 1.4% in May, core-core slowed to 1.8%, its weakest since September 2022, with fuel subsidies masking building pipeline pressure Finance Minister Katayama warned of decisive action against speculative yen moves, pulling USD/JPY back toward 161.00; traders are on guard for actual intervention given thin Juneteenth liquidity ahead The Nikkei slipped modestly on Friday but closed out a strong week; Hong Kong and mainland China markets were shut for a holiday Sterling faced mild headwinds from speculation over a potential leadership challenge against Prime Minister Starmer The dominant story across Friday's session was the swift unravelling of the US-Iran peace process, just two days after the memorandum of understanding was signed. Iran has frozen its MOU commitments and postponed its Geneva meeting with the US, per Fars news agency, citing Israel's continued military operations in Lebanon as a violation of the deal's first clause. Tehran has made clear it will not implement its own obligations until Washington ensures compliance on the Lebanon front. JD Vance's cancellation of his Switzerland trip, first reported by CNN and later confirmed by the White House, was the visible symptom of a process that had already stalled before it formally began. This is the Lebanon tripwire detonating on schedule. Iran warned consistently that an unresolved Lebanon meant a broken deal, and it is now acting on that position. There are hopes of salvaging talks with a potential meeting between lower level officials. Oil and the dollar edged higher during the session as the risk premium that had drained from crude following the Hormuz reopening began to rebuild. Gold slipped back under $4,200. In Japan, May CPI data confirmed what subsidies have been doing to the headline numbers. Core held at 1.4% and core-core slowed to its weakest since September 2022, but producer price acceleration since March signals the pipeline is filling. Finance Minister Katayama added to the yen defence rhetoric, warning of decisive action against speculative moves and pulling USD/JPY back toward 161.00. With Juneteenth thinning US liquidity into the weekend, traders noted the precedent of Japan's late April and early May interventions, which totalled around $72 billion, conducted precisely during holiday-thinned conditions. The Nikkei gave back a fraction of what had been a strong week. Sterling softened modestly on domestic political noise around a potential challenge to Prime Minister Starmer. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 日本5月CPI涨幅依然温和，补贴掩盖了不断加剧的通胀压力 受范斯取消赴伊朗谈判行程的消息影响，油价和美元汇率双双上涨 日本央行副行长表示，对价格风险的拖延可能造成长期 经济损害 范斯取消赴瑞士之行 日本财务大臣表示准备对投机性外汇交易采取果断行动 日本央行副行长警告称，日元汇率波动目前对通胀的影响比过去更大 中国表示6月18日澳大利亚牛肉进口量已达配额的100% 日本央行4月会议纪要： 三名委员希望在4月采取行动；其余委员在6月跟进 日本5月通胀数据：整体通胀同比1.5%（预期1.5%） 核心通胀1.4%（预期1.4%） 英国消费者信心指数表面平稳，实则暗藏裂痕 新西兰2026年5月贸易数据：进出口较4月双双增长 美元兑日元自161.80水平起谜之抛售，交易员纷纷寻找答案 花旗预计2027年第一季度油价将升至60-65美元，因霍尔木兹海峡油流恢复正常 伊拉克表示霍尔木兹海峡重开后将逐步恢复石油出口，但风险犹存 提醒 - 香港和中国市场今日（2026年6月19日）因节假日休市 经过15年的“言论解读”，市场或将受益于美联储“少说多做”的投资策略 Li 美洲外汇新闻综述 6月18日 美元兑日元飙升至2024年以来的高位 摘要：伊朗已冻结其谅解备忘录（MOU）中的承诺，并将与美国的日内瓦会谈推迟至黎巴嫩停火生效之后，以色列的持续袭击触发了该协议的第一项条款；随后万斯取消了瑞士之行，受此消息影响，油价和美元小幅走高； 黄金回落至4,200美元下方；日本5月核心CPI维持在1.4%，核心-核心通胀率放缓至1.8%，创2022年9月以来新低，燃料补贴掩盖了通胀压力积聚的迹象；财务大臣片山警告将对日元投机性波动采取果断行动，推动美元/日元回落至161.00附近； 鉴于“六月节”假期前流动性清淡，交易员正警惕实际干预行动。日经指数周五小幅回落，但本周整体表现强劲；香港和中国内地市场因假期休市。受关于首相斯塔默可能面临领导层挑战的猜测影响，英镑面临温和阻力。周五交易时段的主要焦点是美伊和平进程的迅速破裂，距离谅解备忘录签署仅过去两天。 据法尔斯通讯社报道，伊朗已冻结其谅解备忘录中的承诺，并推迟了与美国在日内瓦的会晤，理由是以色列在黎巴嫩持续开展的军事行动违反了该协议的第一条条款。德黑兰方面已明确表示，在华盛顿确保在黎巴嫩问题上遵守协议之前，伊朗不会履行自身义务。JD·万斯取消瑞士之行——该消息最初由CNN报道，随后得到白宫证实——是这一进程在正式开始前就已经陷入停滞的明显征兆。这是黎巴嫩问题这根“导火索”按计划引爆了。伊朗一再警告，黎巴嫩问题若得不到解决，协议便告破裂，而它现在正据此采取行动。人们希望通过低级别官员的潜在会晤来挽救谈判。受霍尔木兹海峡重开后从原油价格中消退的风险溢价开始回升的影响，本交易时段油价和美元汇率小幅走高。金价回落至4,200美元以下。在日本，5月份的CPI数据证实了补贴措施对整体通胀数据的影响。 核心通胀率维持在1.4%，核心-核心通胀率放缓至2022年9月以来的最低水平，但自3月以来生产者价格涨幅的加快表明通胀传导渠道正在充实。财务大臣片山重信进一步强化了捍卫日元的表态，警告将对投机行为采取果断行动，促使美元兑日元汇率回落至161.00附近。随着“六月节”假期导致周末前美国市场流动性趋紧，交易员们注意到日本4月底至5月初干预市场的先例——当时正值假期导致市场流动性稀薄之际，干预规模总计约720亿美元。日经指数回吐了本周强劲涨幅中的一小部分。受国内政治动荡影响，市场对首相斯塔默可能面临挑战的担忧加剧，英镑小幅走软。本文作者为investinglive.com的伊蒙·谢里丹。
items.summaryEn: Japan CPI stays muted in May as subsidies mask building inflation pressure Oil and US dollar rising on news that Vance cancelled his trip to negotiate with Iran BoJ deputy governor says delaying on price risks could cause long-term economic damage Vance cancels his trip to Switzerland Japan finmin says prepared to take decisive action on speculative FX moves BoJ's Deputy governor warns yen moves now carry bigger inflation punch than in the past China says Australian beef imports hit 100% of quota on June 18th BoJ April minutes: Three members wanted to move in April; the rest caught up by June Japan May inflation data: Headline 1.5% y/y (expected 1.5%) Core 1.4% (expected 1.4%) UK consumer confidence masks growing cracks beneath steady headline New Zealand May 2026 trade data, exports and imports both up from April USD/JPY mystery sell-off from 161.80 leaves traders hunting for answers Citi sees oil at $60-65 by Q1 2027 as Hormuz flows normalise Iraq flags gradual oil return as Hormuz reopens, but risks linger Reminder - Hong Kong and China markets are closed for a holiday today, 19 June 2026 After 15 years of word-watching, markets may benefit from a Fed that talks less investingLive Americas FX news wrap 18 Jun USDJPY surges to near highs going to 2024 Summary: Iran has frozen its MOU commitments and postponed the Geneva talks with the US until a Lebanon ceasefire is in place, with Israel's continued attacks triggering the deal's first clause; Vance's Switzerland cancellation followed Oil and the USD edged higher on the news; gold slipped back under $4,200 Japan's core CPI held at 1.4% in May, core-core slowed to 1.8%, its weakest since September 2022, with fuel subsidies masking building pipeline pressure Finance Minister Katayama warned of decisive action against speculative yen moves, pulling USD/JPY back toward 161.00; traders are on guard for actual intervention given thin Juneteenth liquidity ahead The Nikkei slipped modestly on Friday but closed out a strong week; Hong Kong and mainland China markets were shut for a holiday Sterling faced mild headwinds from speculation over a potential leadership challenge against Prime Minister Starmer The dominant story across Friday's session was the swift unravelling of the US-Iran peace process, just two days after the memorandum of understanding was signed. Iran has frozen its MOU commitments and postponed its Geneva meeting with the US, per Fars news agency, citing Israel's continued military operations in Lebanon as a violation of the deal's first clause. Tehran has made clear it will not implement its own obligations until Washington ensures compliance on the Lebanon front. JD Vance's cancellation of his Switzerland trip, first reported by CNN and later confirmed by the White House, was the visible symptom of a process that had already stalled before it formally began. This is the Lebanon tripwire detonating on schedule. Iran warned consistently that an unresolved Lebanon meant a broken deal, and it is now acting on that position. There are hopes of salvaging talks with a potential meeting between lower level officials. Oil and the dollar edged higher during the session as the risk premium that had drained from crude following the Hormuz reopening began to rebuild. Gold slipped back under $4,200. In Japan, May CPI data confirmed what subsidies have been doing to the headline numbers. Core held at 1.4% and core-core slowed to its weakest since September 2022, but producer price acceleration since March signals the pipeline is filling. Finance Minister Katayama added to the yen defence rhetoric, warning of decisive action against speculative moves and pulling USD/JPY back toward 161.00. With Juneteenth thinning US liquidity into the weekend, traders noted the precedent of Japan's late April and early May interventions, which totalled around $72 billion, conducted precisely during holiday-thinned conditions. The Nikkei gave back a fraction of what had been a strong week. Sterling softened modestly on domestic political noise around a potential challenge to Prime Minister Starmer. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
